seo.meta_title: WorkOS vs Auth.js for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Detailed WorkOS vs Auth.js comparison for Next.js App Router. See how they stack up on pricing, developer experience, and real-world trade-offs.
seo.og_title: WorkOS vs Auth.js: Next.js Auth Comparison (2026)
seo.og_description: WorkOS (4★, Advanced) and Auth.js (4.6★, Medium) compared for Next.js. Who wins on setup, pricing, and DX?

hero.headline: WorkOS vs Auth.js for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of WorkOS and Auth.js — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Here's what WorkOS does better than Auth.js  –  and vice versa. Auth.js wins on Next.js integration (8/10). WorkOS fights back with Custom pricing (enterprise) pricing and No free tier (enterprise sales). For most apps, Auth.js is the smarter pick, but your budget may say otherwise.
verdict_text: We recommend Auth.js for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 20 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. WorkOS is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need no free tier (enterprise sales) or prefer WorkOS's approach to authentication.

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

faq.question: Should I choose WorkOS or Auth.js for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. WorkOS scores 5/10 for Next.js integration with a Advanced learning curve. Auth.js scores 8/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if No free tier (enterprise sales) or Yes, fully open source is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Auth.js more affordable?
faq.answer: WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Auth.js offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Auth.js easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Auth.js is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use WorkOS and Auth.js together?
faq.answer: Using both WorkOS and Auth.js in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: WorkOS or Auth.js?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 5/10, and a user rating of 4★. Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, 8/10, and 4.6★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
